raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A general section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap framing, and contribution claim are coherent at the section level.","R1.2":"The reviewer is expected to check whether the architecture description, stability guarantee claim, ambiguity handling mechanism, and conclusions form a consistent argument chain.","R1.3":"The section reports experiments and uses them to support claims about robustness, naturalness, and deployment practicality, so claim-evidence alignment is central.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence-level clarity, flow, concision, and register are central responsibilities.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended and about what can be judged from the excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The review responsibility includes watching for claims that exceed the reported methods or evidence, especially around formal guarantees and transferability.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within review of the provided section and not execute edits, rewrite the artifact, or evaluate unstated materials.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns robot teleoperation and stability guarantees, the review should be alert to claims whose misuse could affect downstream safety expectations.","RX.5":"Any review-relevant responsibility should be traceable to specific statements in the provided section rather than unsupported assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.1: A general section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap framing, and contribution claim are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to check whether the architecture description, stability guarantee claim, ambiguity handling mechanism, and conclusions form a consistent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section reports experiments and uses them to support claims about robustness, naturalness, and deployment practicality, so claim-evidence alignment is central.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence-level clarity, flow, concision, and register are central responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended and about what can be judged from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes watching for claims that exceed the reported methods or evidence, especially around formal guarantees and transferability.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within review of the provided section and not execute edits, rewrite the artifact, or evaluate unstated materials.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns robot teleoperation and stability guarantees, the review should be alert to claims whose misuse could affect downstream safety expectations.
rationale.RX.5: Any review-relevant responsibility should be traceable to specific statements in the provided section rather than unsupported assumptions.
